raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/scrub> 2024, a purchase was made from the website XXXX for {$140.00} using my Citi Bank credit card. The purchase was for 10 womens vintageXX/XX/XXXX bags. The bags arrived in horrible condition, all peeling and smelling like chemicals. The bags were listed as Grade C with no explanation to what a Grade C was, and only a zoomed out video showcasing the bags with no way of seeing the amount of damage shown. I filed a chargeback with Citi Bank, who closed the dispute in XXXX favor without requesting any proof from me whatsoever. 
Citi Bank reversed my credit and wants me to return the items, which XX/XX/XXXX XXXX initially had denied my attempt for a return in XXXX. When I requested XXXX allow me to return the items once again, XXXX cited that I must abide by XX/XX/XXXX return policy, but I did not use XXXX for my purchase. I used Citi Bank. XXXX only policy on returns states that they will cover the shipping cost for damaged items, however when I reached out to XXXX they told me they would not cover shipping. Shipping costs no less than {$100.00}, which would be more than half of the cost of the refund. Why should I pay {$100.00} out of pocket for extremely damaged goods?
